title: laggera gracilis
intro: Laggera gracilis, also known as Chinese parsley or Vietnamese coriander, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. It is a tropical plant native to Southeast Asia and is commonly used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Laggera gracilis include reducing fever, relieving headaches, and treating respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Traditionally, Laggera gracilis is prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in cooking and as a garnish in various Southeast Asian cuisines.
